test_cases.description: Verifies that internal/db/db.go defines a `teams` table and `team_members` table with a `team_id` FK column, matching the previously defined schema structure.
preconditions: internal/db/db.go source file is present
Go toolchain is available
test_cases.input: Parse the DDL strings from internal/db/db.go
test_cases.expected: DDL contains `CREATE TABLE teams`, `CREATE TABLE team_members`, and `team_id` FK references on both `team_members` and `api_keys` tables
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ddl = read_file('internal/db/db.go')
assert contains(ddl, 'CREATE TABLE teams')
assert contains(ddl, 'CREATE TABLE team_members')
assert contains(ddl, 'team_id') in team_members DDL
assert contains(ddl, 'team_id') in api_keys DDL
assert NOT contains(ddl, 'CREATE TABLE workspaces')
assert NOT contains(ddl, 'CREATE TABLE workspace_members')
assert NOT contains(ddl, 'workspace_id')

test_cases.description: Verifies that internal/db/db.go contains no references to legacy table or column names: `workspaces`, `workspace_members`, or `workspace_id`.
preconditions: internal/db/db.go source file is present
test_cases.input: Read the full content of internal/db/db.go
test_cases.expected: No matches for `workspaces`, `workspace_members`, or `workspace_id` in any DDL string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('internal/db/db.go')
assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bworkspaces\b')
assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bworkspace_members\b')
assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bworkspace_id\b')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the schema initialisation function successfully creates the `teams` and `team_members` tables in a fresh SQLite database without returning an error.
preconditions: In-memory or temp-file SQLite database is available
internal/db/db.go is compiled and the schema init function is accessible
test_cases.input: Call the schema initialisation function with a fresh SQLite connection
test_cases.expected: No error returned; SQLite PRAGMA table_info shows `teams` and `team_members` tables exist with expected columns
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db = open_sqlite(':memory:')
err = db.InitSchema()
assert err == nil
tables = db.query('SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type="table"')
assert 'teams' in tables
assert 'team_members' in tables
assert 'api_keys' in tables
team_cols = db.query('PRAGMA table_info(teams)')
assert column_names(team_cols) includes expected columns
team_member_cols = db.query('PRAGMA table_info(team_members)')
assert 'team_id' in column_names(team_member_cols)
api_key_cols = db.query('PRAGMA table_info(api_keys)')
assert 'team_id' in column_names(api_key_cols)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the store package exports `store.Team` and `store.TeamMember` structs with correct fields, and that `APIKey.TeamID` replaces the old `WorkspaceID` field.
preconditions: Go source files internal/store/teams.go and internal/store/team_members.go are present and compiled
test_cases.input: Inspect exported struct fields of store.Team, store.TeamMember, and store.APIKey via reflection or compilation check
test_cases.expected: `store.Team` exports all required organizational-boundary fields; `store.TeamMember` exports membership fields; `store.APIKey` has `TeamID` field and no `WorkspaceID` field

test_cases.description: Verifies that the store package exports all required CRUD methods with Team-prefixed names and identical behaviour to their Workspace-prefixed predecessors.
preconditions: Go source files internal/store/teams.go and internal/store/team_members.go are compiled
In-memory SQLite database initialised with `teams` and `team_members` tables
test_cases.input: Call each store method: CreateTeam, GetTeamByID, ListTeams, ArchiveTeam, ReactivateTeam, DeleteTeam, AddTeamMember, RemoveTeamMember
test_cases.expected: Each method exists and executes without compilation error; basic CRUD operations return expected results
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db = open_test_db()
team, err = store.CreateTeam(db, {Name: 'Acme'})
assert err == nil AND team.ID != ''
fetched, err = store.GetTeamByID(db, team.ID)
assert err == nil AND fetched.Name == 'Acme'
teams, err = store.ListTeams(db)
assert err == nil AND len(teams) >= 1
err = store.ArchiveTeam(db, team.ID)
assert err == nil
err = store.ReactivateTeam(db, team.ID)
assert err == nil
err = store.AddTeamMember(db, team.ID, user_id)
assert err == nil
err = store.RemoveTeamMember(db, team.ID, user_id)
assert err == nil
err = store.DeleteTeam(db, team.ID)
assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Go codebase contains no exported or unexported identifiers using the `Workspace` or `workspace` prefix for the organizational-boundary concept.
preconditions: Full Go source tree is available excluding spec packages 01-04
test_cases.input: Grep or AST scan of all .go files under the project root
test_cases.expected: Zero matches for `Workspace`-prefixed identifiers or `workspace`-prefixed identifiers used for the organizational-boundary concept
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
files = glob('**/*.go', exclude=['specs/01', 'specs/02', 'specs/03', 'specs/04'])
for file in files:
    content = read_file(file)
    assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bWorkspace[A-Z]')
    assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bworkspace[A-Z_]') // catches workspace_id, workspaceID etc.
assert exit_code(run('go build ./...')) == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that team_handler.go defines a `TeamHandler` struct with all required handler methods.
preconditions: team_handler.go is present at its package path and compiled
test_cases.input: Inspect the Go source file team_handler.go for exported struct and method names
test_cases.expected: `TeamHandler` struct is defined; methods `CreateTeam`, `ListTeams`, `GetTeam`, `ArchiveTeam`, `ReactivateTeam`, `DeleteTeam`, `AddTeamMember`, `RemoveTeamMember` are defined on it
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
source = read_file('team_handler.go')
assert contains(source, 'type TeamHandler struct')
for method in ['CreateTeam','ListTeams','GetTeam','ArchiveTeam','ReactivateTeam','DeleteTeam','AddTeamMember','RemoveTeamMember']:
    assert regex_match(source, r'func.*TeamHandler.*' + method)
assert NOT contains(source, 'WorkspaceHandler')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the auth middleware uses `ContextKeyTeamID` to store and retrieve the authenticated team ID from the Echo request context.
preconditions: Auth middleware source file is compiled
Echo framework is available
test_cases.input: Inspect auth middleware source for context key usage
test_cases.expected: `ContextKeyTeamID` is defined and used; `ContextKeyWorkspaceID` is absent from all middleware files
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
files = glob('internal/**/*.go')
combined = concat(read_file(f) for f in files)
assert contains(combined, 'ContextKeyTeamID')
assert NOT contains(combined, 'ContextKeyWorkspaceID')
// Integration check: simulate middleware sets context key
ctx = create_echo_context()
middleware.SetTeamID(ctx, 'team-123')
assert get_context_value(ctx, ContextKeyTeamID) == 'team-123'

test_cases.description: Verifies that POST /api/v1/teams creates a new team and returns HTTP 201 with a JSON body containing `team_id`.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
SQLite database is initialised with `teams` table
test_cases.input: HTTP POST request to /api/v1/teams with JSON body {"name": "Alpha"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 response; JSON body contains `team_id` field with a non-empty value; team row inserted in `teams` table
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/teams', body={"name": "Alpha"})
assert resp.status_code == 201
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert 'team_id' in body OR body['id'] != ''
assert NOT 'workspace_id' in body
team = db.query('SELECT * FROM teams WHERE name = "Alpha"')
assert team != None

test_cases.description: Verifies that GET /api/v1/teams returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of team objects.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
At least one team record exists in the `teams` table
test_cases.input: HTTP GET request to /api/v1/teams
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 response; JSON array containing at least one team object; no `workspace_id` fields in the array elements
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_get('/api/v1/teams')
assert resp.status_code == 200
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert is_array(body)
assert len(body) >= 1
for item in body:
    assert NOT 'workspace_id' in item

test_cases.description: Verifies that GET /api/v1/teams/:id returns HTTP 200 with the matching team JSON object, or HTTP 404 if not found.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
A team with a known ID exists in the `teams` table
test_cases.input: HTTP GET request to /api/v1/teams/{existing_id} and /api/v1/teams/nonexistent-id
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with team JSON for existing ID; HTTP 404 for nonexistent ID
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_get('/api/v1/teams/' + existing_id)
assert resp.status_code == 200
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body['id'] == existing_id OR body['team_id'] == existing_id
resp404 = http_get('/api/v1/teams/nonexistent-id')
assert resp404.status_code == 404

test_cases.description: Verifies that PUT /api/v1/teams/:id/archive archives the team and returns HTTP 200.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
An active team with a known ID exists in the `teams` table
test_cases.input: HTTP PUT request to /api/v1/teams/{existing_id}/archive
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 response; team's lifecycle state is archived in the `teams` table
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_put('/api/v1/teams/' + existing_id + '/archive')
assert resp.status_code == 200
team = db.query('SELECT status FROM teams WHERE id = ?', existing_id)
assert team.status == 'archived'

test_cases.description: Verifies that PUT /api/v1/teams/:id/reactivate reactivates an archived team and returns HTTP 200.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
An archived team with a known ID exists in the `teams` table
test_cases.input: HTTP PUT request to /api/v1/teams/{archived_id}/reactivate
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 response; team's lifecycle state is active in the `teams` table
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_put('/api/v1/teams/' + archived_id + '/reactivate')
assert resp.status_code == 200
team = db.query('SELECT status FROM teams WHERE id = ?', archived_id)
assert team.status == 'active'

test_cases.description: Verifies that DELETE /api/v1/teams/:id deletes the team and returns HTTP 204.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
A team with a known ID exists in the `teams` table
test_cases.input: HTTP DELETE request to /api/v1/teams/{existing_id}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 response; team row is removed from the `teams` table
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_delete('/api/v1/teams/' + existing_id)
assert resp.status_code == 204
team = db.query('SELECT * FROM teams WHERE id = ?', existing_id)
assert team == None

test_cases.description: Verifies that POST /api/v1/teams/:id/members adds a member to the team and returns HTTP 201.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
A team and a user both exist in the database
test_cases.input: HTTP POST request to /api/v1/teams/{team_id}/members with JSON body {"user_id": "user-123"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 response; a row is inserted in `team_members` with the correct `team_id` and `user_id`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_post('/api/v1/teams/' + team_id + '/members', body={"user_id": "user-123"})
assert resp.status_code == 201
member = db.query('SELECT * FROM team_members WHERE team_id = ? AND user_id = ?', team_id, 'user-123')
assert member != None

test_cases.description: Verifies that DELETE /api/v1/teams/:id/members/:user_id removes a member from the team and returns HTTP 204.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
A team membership record exists for team_id and user_id in `team_members`
test_cases.input: HTTP DELETE request to /api/v1/teams/{team_id}/members/{user_id}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 response; membership row is removed from `team_members`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_delete('/api/v1/teams/' + team_id + '/members/' + user_id)
assert resp.status_code == 204
member = db.query('SELECT * FROM team_members WHERE team_id = ? AND user_id = ?', team_id, user_id)
assert member == None

test_cases.description: Verifies that all API JSON responses and request bodies use `team_id` and never `workspace_id` for the organizational-boundary identifier.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with team routes registered
SQLite database is initialised
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/teams (create team), POST /api/v1/keys (create API key scoped to team)
test_cases.expected: All JSON response bodies contain `team_id` key; none contain `workspace_id`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
create_resp = http_post('/api/v1/teams', body={"name": "Beta"})
team_body = parse_json(create_resp.body)
assert 'workspace_id' NOT in team_body
key_resp = http_post('/api/v1/keys', body={"team_id": team_body['id']})
key_body = parse_json(key_resp.body)
assert 'team_id' in key_body
assert 'workspace_id' NOT in key_body

test_cases.description: Verifies that no routes are registered under the `/api/v1/workspaces` path prefix in the Echo router setup.
preconditions: Echo router setup code is compiled and accessible
test_cases.input: Inspect router registration source code and/or query registered routes at runtime
test_cases.expected: No route entries with path containing `/workspaces` are registered

test_cases.description: Verifies that `afc keys create --team <slug>` creates an API key scoped to the specified team, exits 0, and prints output including `team_id`.
preconditions: afc binary is built
A team with the given slug exists in the running server
test_cases.input: Shell command: `afc keys create --team my-team`
test_cases.expected: Process exits with code 0; stdout contains `team_id`; a new API key row with the correct `team_id` is inserted in `api_keys`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_command('afc keys create --team my-team')
assert result.exit_code == 0
assert contains(result.stdout, 'team_id')
assert NOT contains(result.stdout, 'workspace_id')
key = db.query('SELECT team_id FROM api_keys ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1')
assert key.team_id == resolve_team_id('my-team')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the `afc` CLI binary exposes no `--workspace` flag on any subcommand.
preconditions: afc binary is built
test_cases.input: Run `afc --help`, `afc keys --help`, `afc keys create --help` and inspect all flag listings
test_cases.expected: None of the help outputs list a `--workspace` flag
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for cmd in ['afc --help', 'afc keys --help', 'afc keys create --help']:
    result = run_command(cmd)
    assert NOT contains(result.stdout, '--workspace')
    assert NOT contains(result.stderr, '--workspace')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Go source tree contains the renamed files: team_handler.go, teams.go, and team_members.go at their expected package paths.
preconditions: Go source tree is checked out
test_cases.input: File system check for expected file names
test_cases.expected: All three renamed files exist at their package paths
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert file_exists('internal/api/team_handler.go') OR file_exists(find_file('team_handler.go'))
assert file_exists('internal/store/teams.go') OR file_exists(find_file('teams.go'))
assert file_exists('internal/store/team_members.go') OR file_exists(find_file('team_members.go'))

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Go source tree contains no files named workspace_handler.go, workspaces.go, or workspace_members.go.
preconditions: Go source tree is checked out
test_cases.input: File system glob search for legacy file names
test_cases.expected: None of the legacy workspace-prefixed source files exist
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert NOT file_exists(find_file('workspace_handler.go'))
assert NOT file_exists(find_file('workspaces.go'))
assert NOT file_exists(find_file('workspace_members.go'))

test_cases.description: Verifies that the integration test directory contains team_handler_test.go and team_edge_test.go, and that all test assertions within reference team types and endpoints.
preconditions: Go source tree is checked out
test_cases.input: File system check and grep of integration test directory
test_cases.expected: Both renamed test files exist; neither file references `/api/v1/workspaces` or workspace-prefixed types
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert file_exists('internal/integration/team_handler_test.go')
assert file_exists('internal/integration/team_edge_test.go')
for f in ['internal/integration/team_handler_test.go', 'internal/integration/team_edge_test.go']:
    content = read_file(f)
    assert NOT contains(content, '/api/v1/workspaces')
    assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bWorkspace[A-Z]')
assert NOT file_exists('internal/integration/workspace_handler_test.go')
assert NOT file_exists('internal/integration/workspace_edge_test.go')

test_cases.description: Verifies that docs/api.md reflects all renamed endpoint paths and uses `team_id` in all request/response examples.
preconditions: docs/api.md exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and inspect the content of docs/api.md
test_cases.expected: All endpoint paths use /api/v1/teams; all JSON examples use `team_id`; no `/api/v1/workspaces` or `workspace_id` present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('docs/api.md')
assert contains(content, '/api/v1/teams')
assert contains(content, 'team_id')
assert NOT contains(content, '/api/v1/workspaces')
assert NOT contains(content, 'workspace_id')

test_cases.description: Verifies that docs/cli.md documents the `--team` flag for `afc keys create` and contains no references to `--workspace`.
preconditions: docs/cli.md exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and inspect the content of docs/cli.md
test_cases.expected: `--team` flag is documented; no `--workspace` flag reference exists
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('docs/cli.md')
assert contains(content, '--team')
assert NOT contains(content, '--workspace')

test_cases.description: Verifies that docs/architecture.md uses 'team' for the organizational-boundary entity and contains no occurrences of 'workspace' referring to that concept.
preconditions: docs/architecture.md exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and inspect the content of docs/architecture.md
test_cases.expected: Organizational entity is consistently referred to as 'team'; no occurrences of 'workspace' in the organizational-boundary sense
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('docs/architecture.md')
assert contains(content, 'team')
// Allow 'workspace' only if it refers to the upcoming workspace_entity concept with explicit context
workspace_count = count_occurrences(content, 'workspace')
assert workspace_count == 0 OR all_occurrences_are_scoped_to_workspace_entity(content)

test_cases.description: Verifies that docs/configuration.md, docs/errata/*.md, and README.md replace all organizational 'workspace' occurrences with 'team'.
preconditions: docs/configuration.md, README.md exist; docs/errata/ directory is present if non-empty
test_cases.input: Read and inspect docs/configuration.md, README.md, and all files matching docs/errata/*.md
test_cases.expected: No occurrences of 'workspace' in the organizational-boundary sense in any of those files

test_cases.description: Verifies that the full test suite passes with zero failures after the rename by running `go test ./...`.
preconditions: All source files have been renamed and updated
SQLite database initialisation uses `teams` schema
Go toolchain is available
test_cases.input: Run `go test ./...` from the project root
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; no test failures or compilation errors reported
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_command('go test ./...')
assert result.exit_code == 0
assert NOT contains(result.stdout, 'FAIL')
assert NOT contains(result.stderr, 'FAIL')

test_cases.description: Verifies that RBAC enforcement, team lifecycle states, and API key scoping logic are behaviourally identical to the pre-rename implementation.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with renamed routes
SQLite database is initialised with renamed schema
test_cases.input: Execute RBAC-protected operations, lifecycle state transitions (active→archived→active), and API key scoping scenarios
test_cases.expected: RBAC denies unauthorised access; lifecycle transitions succeed in correct order; API keys are scoped to the correct team_id — all behaviour identical to pre-rename
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// RBAC check
unauth_resp = http_get('/api/v1/teams', headers={})
assert unauth_resp.status_code == 401 OR 403
// Lifecycle
team = create_team('LifecycleTest')
err = archive_team(team.id)
assert err == nil
err = reactivate_team(team.id)
assert err == nil
// API key scoping
key = create_api_key(team_id=team.id)
assert key.TeamID == team.id
// Use key for another team's resource
bad_resp = http_get('/api/v1/teams/' + other_team.id, headers={'Authorization': key.value})
assert bad_resp.status_code in [401, 403]

test_cases.description: Verifies that the renamed codebase produces a clean build with exit code 0 from both `go build ./...` and `go vet ./...`.
preconditions: All source files have been renamed and updated
Go toolchain is available
test_cases.input: Run `go build ./...` and `go vet ./...` from the project root
test_cases.expected: Both commands exit with code 0; no errors or warnings
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
build_result = run_command('go build ./...')
assert build_result.exit_code == 0
assert build_result.stderr == ''
vet_result = run_command('go vet ./...')
assert vet_result.exit_code == 0
assert vet_result.stderr == ''

test_cases.description: Verifies that spec packages 01–04 and their associated artifacts are unmodified by this rename.
preconditions: Git history is available
Spec packages 01-04 artifacts are present
test_cases.input: Run `git diff HEAD~1 -- specs/01 specs/02 specs/03 specs/04` (or equivalent path check)
test_cases.expected: No changes to any files in spec packages 01, 02, 03, or 04

property_tests.description: For any Go source file in the repository (excluding spec packages 01-04), the file contains no exported or unexported identifiers using the `Workspace` or `workspace` prefix for the organizational-boundary concept.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Each .go file path returned by glob('**/*.go') excluding paths under specs/01, specs/02, specs/03, specs/04
property_tests.invariant_check:
content = read_file(file_path)
assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bWorkspace[A-Z]') // exported Workspace-prefixed identifiers
assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bworkspace_id\b') // snake_case field name
assert NOT regex_match(content, r'\bworkspace[A-Z]') // camelCase unexported identifiers like workspaceCount
// Allow the string literal 'workspace' only in comments explaining the rename rationale

property_tests.description: For any DDL string in internal/db/db.go, the string contains no references to legacy table or column names `workspaces`, `workspace_members`, or `workspace_id`.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Each SQL DDL string literal extracted from internal/db/db.go
property_tests.invariant_check:
for each ddl_string in extract_string_literals('internal/db/db.go'):
    assert NOT regex_match(ddl_string, r'\bworkspaces\b')
    assert NOT regex_match(ddl_string, r'\bworkspace_members\b')
    assert NOT regex_match(ddl_string, r'\bworkspace_id\b')
    // Positive assertion: check renamed names are present
assert regex_match(all_ddl, r'\bteams\b')
assert regex_match(all_ddl, r'\bteam_members\b')
assert regex_match(all_ddl, r'\bteam_id\b')

property_tests.description: For any route registration call in the Echo HTTP router setup, no route path contains `/workspaces` — all organizational-boundary routes are under `/api/v1/teams`.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Each route object returned by echo_app.Routes() after full router initialisation

property_tests.description: For any JSON response or request body produced or consumed by the server that carries the organizational-boundary identifier, the field is serialised as `team_id` and never as `workspace_id`.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Each HTTP response body collected by calling all /api/v1/teams endpoints (create, list, get, archive, reactivate, add member, create API key)

property_tests.description: For any execution of `go test ./...` on the renamed codebase, every test case passes and the command exits with code 0, confirming zero behavioral regression from the rename.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Each run of `go test ./...` (including with -count=1 to disable caching, and with -race to detect race conditions)
property_tests.invariant_check:
for each run_flags in ['', '-count=1', '-race', '-count=1 -race']:
    result = run_command('go test ' + run_flags + ' ./...')
    assert result.exit_code == 0
    assert NOT regex_match(result.stdout, r'^--- FAIL')
    assert NOT regex_match(result.stdout, r'^FAIL\s')
    assert result.stderr == '' OR result.stderr contains only race detector summary with 0 races

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a grep/AST scan of internal/db/db.go for legacy names (`workspaces`, `workspace_members`, `workspace_id`) returns a non-zero exit code if any residual reference exists.
preconditions: internal/db/db.go is present
grep or equivalent tool is available in CI environment
edge_case_tests.input: Run grep for legacy names in internal/db/db.go
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1 (no match found) when the file is correctly renamed; if any legacy name exists, the grep exits 0 (match found) indicating CI failure

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that introducing a reference to a deleted Workspace-prefixed type causes `go build ./...` to fail with a non-zero exit code.
preconditions: Go toolchain is available
Renamed codebase is present
edge_case_tests.input: Temporarily inject `var _ = store.Workspace{}` into a Go file and run `go build ./...`
edge_case_tests.expected: Non-zero exit code from `go build ./...` with an error referencing the undefined `store.Workspace` symbol
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Inject stale reference
original = read_file('internal/store/teams.go')
write_file('internal/store/teams.go', original + '\nvar _ = Workspace{}')
build_result = run_command('go build ./...')
assert build_result.exit_code != 0
assert contains(build_result.stderr, 'undefined') OR contains(build_result.stderr, 'Workspace')
// Restore
write_file('internal/store/teams.go', original)

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a grep-based or AST-based CI check reports a violation when any residual workspace-prefixed identifier exists in the codebase.
preconditions: Full Go source tree is accessible
CI lint script is available
edge_case_tests.input: Run the CI grep/AST check against the renamed codebase; then run against a version with an injected `workspaceCount` variable
edge_case_tests.expected: Clean codebase: check exits non-zero (no matches); injected violation: check exits 0 (match found), causing CI failure
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Clean check
clean_result = run_command('grep -rn --include="*.go" -E "\bWorkspace[A-Z]|\bworkspace[A-Z_]" . --exclude-dir=specs')
assert clean_result.exit_code != 0 // no matches found
// Violation check
original = read_file('internal/store/teams.go')
write_file('internal/store/teams.go', original + '\nvar workspaceCount = 0')
violation_result = run_command('grep -rn --include="*.go" -E "\bworkspace[A-Z_]" . --exclude-dir=specs')
assert violation_result.exit_code == 0 // match found → CI failure
write_file('internal/store/teams.go', original)

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that any request to a legacy /api/v1/workspaces path returns HTTP 404 with no redirect.
preconditions: Echo HTTP server is running with only /api/v1/teams routes registered
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404 Not Found for all legacy paths; no HTTP 301/302 redirect response; no workspace handler invoked
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
legacy_paths = ['/api/v1/workspaces', '/api/v1/workspaces/abc', '/api/v1/workspaces/abc/archive', '/api/v1/workspaces/abc/members']
for path in legacy_paths:
    resp = http_get(path)
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    assert resp.status_code NOT in [301, 302, 307, 308]

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that passing `--workspace` flag to `afc keys create` returns an unknown flag error, exits non-zero, and performs no API call.
preconditions: afc binary is built
No API server is required (error should occur before any network call)
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with non-zero exit code; stderr or stdout contains 'unknown flag' or 'unknown flag: --workspace'; no API key is created
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_command('afc keys create --workspace my-workspace')
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert contains(result.stderr, 'unknown flag') OR contains(result.stderr, '--workspace')
// Confirm no API call was made (e.g. mock server received no requests)
assert api_server_request_count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a test referencing a deleted workspace-prefixed type causes `go test ./...` to fail at the compilation stage.
preconditions: Go toolchain is available
Renamed codebase is present
edge_case_tests.input: Temporarily inject `var _ = store.WorkspaceMember{}` into a test file and run `go test ./...`
edge_case_tests.expected: Non-zero exit from `go test ./...` with a compilation error referencing the undefined `store.WorkspaceMember` symbol
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
test_file = 'internal/integration/team_handler_test.go'
original = read_file(test_file)
write_file(test_file, original + '\nvar _ = store.WorkspaceMember{}')
test_result = run_command('go test ./...')
assert test_result.exit_code != 0
assert contains(test_result.stderr, 'undefined') OR contains(test_result.stderr, 'WorkspaceMember')
write_file(test_file, original)

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for creating a team via the renamed POST /api/v1/teams endpoint, verifying the full path from HTTP request through Echo router, TeamHandler, store CreateTeam, teams table insert, and HTTP 201 JSON response with team_id.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send HTTP POST /api/v1/teams with JSON body {"name": "Smoke Test Team"}
real_components: Echo HTTP router with /api/v1/teams routes registered
mockable: Authentication middleware (can use a test API key or bypass for smoke test)
expected_effects: Response JSON body contains `team_id` field with a non-empty UUID
Response JSON body does NOT contain `workspace_id`
A new row exists in the `teams` SQLite table matching the submitted name

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for creating an API key scoped to a team via the renamed `afc keys create --team` CLI flag, verifying the full path from CLI invocation through the --team flag, team_id payload, API server, api_keys table insert, and stdout output.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run shell command: `afc keys create --team smoke-team` with a running API server and a pre-created team with slug 'smoke-team'
real_components: afc CLI binary (Cobra command with --team flag)
API server endpoint for key creation
mockable: API server HTTP layer (can use httptest.Server for isolation)
expected_effects: stdout contains `team_id` with the correct team's ID
A new row is inserted in `api_keys` with `team_id` matching the smoke-team's ID
No `--workspace` flag appears in `afc keys create --help` output

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for archiving a team via the renamed PUT /api/v1/teams/:id/archive endpoint, verifying the full path from HTTP request through Echo router, TeamHandler.ArchiveTeam, store ArchiveTeam, and teams table state update.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send HTTP PUT /api/v1/teams/{team_id}/archive for a pre-existing active team
real_components: Echo HTTP router with PUT /api/v1/teams/:id/archive route
mockable: Authentication middleware (use test API key scoped to the target team)
expected_effects: The team's status/lifecycle field in the `teams` SQLite table is updated to 'archived'
Subsequent GET /api/v1/teams/{team_id} returns the team with archived status

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for adding a member to a team via the renamed POST /api/v1/teams/:id/members endpoint, verifying the full path from HTTP request through Echo router, TeamHandler.AddTeamMember, store.AddTeamMember, and team_members table insert with team_id.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send HTTP POST /api/v1/teams/{team_id}/members with JSON body {"user_id": "user-smoke-456"}
real_components: Echo HTTP router with POST /api/v1/teams/:id/members route
mockable: Authentication middleware (use test API key scoped to the target team)
expected_effects: A new row is inserted in the `team_members` SQLite table with `team_id` matching the target team and `user_id` = 'user-smoke-456'
